2603.16664.title: Kestrel: Grounding Self-Refinement for LVLM Hallucination Mitigation
2603.16664.paper_summary: Large vision-language models (LVLMs) have become increasingly strong but remain prone to hallucinations in multimodal tasks, which significantly narrows their deployment. As training these LVLMs to avoid hallucinations becomes prohibitively expensive for larger models, training-free methods offer a cheap and flexible solution to this problem, yet existing approaches based on decoding or tool use often bring limited gains and/or weak interpretability. We propose Kestrel, a training-free framework for LVLM hallucination mitigation that combines an explicit visual-grounding agent with evidence-verified self-refinement mechanism. In detail, Kestrel first collects explicit visual evidence and converts tool outputs into reusable and structured textual evidence. Second, to take full advantage of these evidence, Kestrel verifies them via an LVLM judge for evidence checking, then iteratively self-refine answers based on verified evidence to reduce the risk of over-correction. Extensive experiments show that Kestrel improves performance over strong baselines across hallucination benchmarks (e.g., average +3.31% on POPE and +28.34 on MME-Hallucination with Qwen3-VL), while providing transparent verification traces for hallucination diagnosis and analysis -- e.g., both the integrated self-refinement module and grounding agent contributing an average +2.0% gain on POPE.
2603.16664.paper_summary_zh: 大型視覺-語言模型（LVLMs）變得越來越強大，但在多模態任務中仍然容易出現幻覺，這顯著限制了它們的部署。由於訓練這些LVLMs以避免幻覺對於較大模型來說變得過於昂貴，因此無需訓練的方法提供了一種便宜且靈活的解決方案，但現有基於解碼或工具使用的方法往往帶來有限的收益和/或較弱的可解釋性。我們提出了Kestrel，一個無需訓練的LVLM幻覺緩解框架，結合了一個明確的視覺基礎代理和經過證據驗證的自我精煉機制。具體而言，Kestrel首先收集明確的視覺證據，並將工具輸出轉換為可重用和結構化的文本證據。其次，為了充分利用這些證據，Kestrel通過LVLM評判者進行證據檢查來驗證它們，然後根據經過驗證的證據迭代自我精煉答案，以減少過度修正的風險。大量實驗表明，Kestrel在幻覺基準測試中相較於強基線提高了性能（例如，在POPE上平均提高+3.31%，在MME-Hallucination上提高+28.34，使用Qwen3-VL），同時提供了透明的驗證痕跡以進行幻覺診斷和分析——例如，集成的自我精煉模組和基礎代理平均貢獻了+2.0%的收益於POPE。
2603.16664.author: Jiawei Mao et.al.
2603.16664.authors: Jiawei Mao, Hardy Chen, Haoqin Tu, Yuhan Wang, Letian Zhang, Zeyu Zheng, Huaxiu Yao, Zirui Wang, Cihang Xie, Yuyin Zhou
